_meta.failure_shape: v2.17.0 Prod-safe test classification — failure mode: a codebase has 4 test files exhibiting each of the 4 named severities. (1) Unannotated test that only reads — `unclassified-test`. (2) Annotated `@prod-safe` but contains `page.request.post` — `mutation-in-prod-safe-test` AND `classification-mismatch`. (3) Annotated `@not-prod-safe` and scheduled against a production URL — `prod-deployment-runs-unsafe-test`. (4) Properly annotated `@prod-safe` clean test against production — passes (no gap).
_meta.originating_failure: verbatim user prose: 'update such that any form of playright and QA testing knows that when deploying to production, any testing must be non-destructive and perform no mutations to any data / no changes. So we will want to ensure this. also we will want every test written to be properly classified into prod safe or not.'

_corrected_verification_artifact._note: Correct shape — all test files in the schedule are @prod-safe; the @not-prod-safe mutation tests (matter/create + upload/document) were filtered out of the run schedule because run_target is production. The login test was annotated and the @prod-safe mutation conflict was resolved by removing the bogus annotation.

_corrected_run_target._note: Same prod URL; the @not-prod-safe upload test is removed from the test_files schedule (the orchestrator filters it for prod runs).
